_comment_detail: Based on Phase 1 finding: high layers (18-23) are most sensitive to budget cuts.
Design: fix high layers at their mono_inc base values, apply query-aware only to
low/mid layers (0-17) where budget sensitivity is lower.
Mechanism: budget_min_k == budget_max_k for fixed layers → _compute_dynamic_block_counts
returns self.block_counts unchanged for those layers (no budget_head created).
layers 0-5  : base k=8,  dynamic [4,  16]  ← low sensitivity, free to adjust
layers 6-11 : base k=12, dynamic [8,  24]  ← medium sensitivity
layers 12-17: base k=20, dynamic [8,  24]  ← medium-high sensitivity
layers 18-23: base k=24, FIXED   [24, 24]  ← high sensitivity, locked
Research question: is query-aware useful only in low layers, or does it
require high-layer flexibility to show benefit?
